# Bloomberg Data Refresh — Transient Lab Machine — UltraCode Brief

You are on a UNIVERSITY LAB computer that has the Bloomberg Terminal but NONE of
the project's files, and is TRANSIENT (nothing persists). Goal: pull fresh
Bloomberg data for the smart-wheel-engine repo and get it to GitHub before the
machine is reclaimed. If it isn't pushed, it's lost. You are a senior quant data
engineer: careful, frugal with metered university data, honest about failure.
NEVER fabricate data — if a pull fails or the API is unavailable, say so and fall
back to the manual runbook. This is a data refresh, not an engine change.

Today = 2026-06-02. Committed sp500_ohlcv.csv is stale at 2026-03-20 (missing
window ~50 trading days). Repo: https://github.com/MertYakar66/smart-wheel-engine.git

## STEP 0 — Verify Bloomberg Desktop API FIRST (gate everything on this)
Lab terminals often allow the GUI but NOT the Desktop API. Before heavy work:
1) Confirm the Bloomberg Terminal app is running and LOGGED IN on this machine.
2) In a fresh venv: pip install blpapi xbbg pandas, then:
     from xbbg import blp; print(blp.bdp("SPX Index", "PX_LAST"))
3) Returns a price -> API works -> go to STEP 1.
   Errors (no blpapi / connection refused / no entitlement) -> STOP the automated
   pull, go to MANUAL FALLBACK, do not fabricate data.

## STEP 1 — Set up (automated path)
- git clone --depth 1 https://github.com/MertYakar66/smart-wheel-engine.git
  (You need the user's GitHub auth to push. Use existing creds / gh auth login /
  a PAT. If you can't authenticate, still pull + save locally and tell the user
  exactly how to retrieve — the machine is transient.)
- venv + pip install pandas xbbg blpapi.
- git checkout -b data/bloomberg-refresh-2026-06-02

## STEP 2 — Pull, frugally (mind university data limits)
The Desktop API is metered; a full 503-name x 8-year pull may hit the lab limit.
- Tiny test first (2-3 tickers, last 5 days) to confirm flow + gauge headroom.
- Prefer a DELTA pull (2026-03-20 -> 2026-06-02) merged/deduped onto the existing
  CSV in the clone. Full re-pull only if delta-merge is problematic.

Tier 1 — the stale core (highest priority):
1) OHLCV -> data/bloomberg/sp500_ohlcv.csv. Use scripts/pull_ohlcv.py (change
   end_date "2026-03-20" -> "2026-06-02") or a delta variant. Columns MUST stay
   date,ticker,open,high,low,close,volume.
2) IV -> data/bloomberg/sp500_vol_iv_full.csv (NO script — WRITE one as
   scripts/pull_vol_iv.py mirroring pull_ohlcv.py). Fields for members('SPX Index'):
   hist_put_imp_vol, hist_call_imp_vol, volatility_30d, volatility_60d,
   volatility_90d, volatility_260d. Columns MUST be date,hist_put_imp_vol,
   hist_call_imp_vol,volatility_30d,volatility_60d,volatility_90d,volatility_260d,ticker.
3) Liquidity -> data/bloomberg/sp500_liquidity.csv. Use scripts/pull_liquidity.py
   (update end_date) BUT FIX the schema: committed file is
   date,avg_vol_30d,turnover,shares_out,ticker — add EQY_SH_OUT (shares_out) and
   match that order; do NOT drop shares_out.

Tier 2 — regime/vol context (Bloomberg-unique; helps both engines). Copy queries
from scripts/bloomberg_bql_pulls.md (end date 2026-06-02), run via xbbg, save to
data/bloomberg/ with the columns that doc specifies:
- §4 VIX futures UX1-UX7 -> vix_futures_curve.csv (Theta tier-blocked; Bloomberg-only)
- §5 Vol indices SKEW/VVIX/VIX9D/VXN/RVX -> vol_indices.csv
- §1 Macro calendar FOMC/CPI/NFP -> sp500_macro_calendar.csv (activates event gate)
- §7 Correlation COR1M/3M/6M -> spx_correlation.csv
- §12 Rates/FX vol MOVE/CVIX -> rates_fx_vol.csv
- §3 Short interest -> sp500_short_interest.csv ; §2 Corp actions -> sp500_corporate_actions.csv
- Skip §9 (dealer GEX) — needs a special subscription.

## STEP 3 — Validate
Per CSV: row count, date range reaches 2026-06-02, no all-NaN columns, header
matches committed schema. Best-effort: python scripts/feature_smoke_test.py.

## STEP 4 — Get it off the machine (CRITICAL — transient)
git add data/bloomberg/*.csv scripts/pull_vol_iv.py ; commit (what refreshed,
date ranges, row counts) ; git push -u origin data/bloomberg-refresh-2026-06-02.
If push fails (auth), upload the CSVs somewhere the user can retrieve and report
which files + where. NEVER leave the only copy on this machine.

## STEP 5 — Report
What pulled (file, rows, date range), what failed, data used vs limit, the branch
to merge from home. If API was unavailable, say so and point to the manual runbook.

## Guardrails
- Never fabricate data; on failure fall back to the manual runbook.
- Data refresh + one new pull script only; don't modify engine logic.
- Respect committed CSV schemas (esp. liquidity shares_out).
- Frugal with metered data: test small, prefer delta pulls.
- Commit to a BRANCH and push; never touch main. Don't touch the bot repo here.

## MANUAL FALLBACK (if STEP 0 shows no Desktop API)
Write BLOOMBERG_MANUAL_PULL.md with: the Tier-1 IV BQL query, every Tier-2 query
from scripts/bloomberg_bql_pulls.md (end date 2026-06-02), save-as paths, and how
to commit/push from home. IV query:
=BQL.QUERY("get(hist_put_imp_vol, hist_call_imp_vol, volatility_30d, volatility_60d, volatility_90d, volatility_260d) for(members('SPX Index')) with(dates=range(2015-01-01, 2026-06-02), fill=prev)")
Then stop and tell the user to run them by hand in Excel — do not fake the data.

## claude.ai Figma
The official Figma MCP server. Use this server whenever the user wants to create, generate, edit, implement, or sync any design, UI, screen, component, mockup, or visual — in Figma, FigJam, Figma Make, or Figma Slides — and whenever the user mentions Figma or provides a figma.com URL.

This server bridges code and design in both directions, and supports designing from scratch using existing design systems and codebases.

CAPABILITIES:
- Read designs FROM Figma into code (get_design_context, get_screenshot, get_metadata, get_figjam)
- Write designs INTO Figma from code, intent, or existing components (use_figma, generate_figma_design, create_new_file, upload_assets)
- Bridge code and design via Code Connect (get_code_connect_map, add_code_connect_map, list_code_components, get_code_component_info)
- Create diagrams and FigJam content (generate_diagram, get_figjam)

WHEN TO USE THESE TOOLS:
- The user wants to create, mock up, or generate any UI, screen, component, or design — even if Figma isn't named
- The user wants to implement a design as code (design-to-code)
- The user wants to push a page, view, or component into Figma (code-to-design)
- The user wants to update, sync, or edit an existing Figma file
- The user shares a figma.com URL
- The user wants to build or extend a design system, design tokens, or component library
- The user wants to create a diagram in FigJam

SKILLS (load first when available):
- /figma-use — MANDATORY before calling use_figma
- /figma-generate-design — for translating an app page or layout into Figma
- /figma-generate-library — for building a design system in Figma from code
- /figma-code-connect — for mapping Figma components to codebase components
- /figma-use-figjam — for FigJam-specific use_figma flows
- /figma-generate-diagram — MANDATORY before calling generate_diagram

URL PARSING:
Extract fileKey and nodeId from Figma URLs:
- figma.com/design/:fileKey/:fileName?node-id=:nodeId → convert "-" to ":" in nodeId
- figma.com/design/:fileKey/branch/:branchKey/:fileName → use branc… [truncated]

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

I still need to run the real `blp.bdp` test. The blocker right now is that **real Python isn't installed** (only the MS Store stub). Let me hunt for a usable Python (Anaconda is common on lab machines) plus git/gh for the push later.

file.content:
import os

import pandas as pd
from xbbg import blp

print("Getting S&P 500 members...")
members = blp.bds("SPX Index", "INDX_MWEIGHT")
tickers = [t + " Equity" for t in members["member_ticker_and_exchange_code"].tolist()]
print(f"Found {len(tickers)} tickers")

FIELDS = ["PX_OPEN", "PX_HIGH", "PX_LOW", "PX_LAST", "PX_VOLUME"]
CHUNK_SIZE = 30
all_chunks = []

for i in range(0, len(tickers), CHUNK_SIZE):
    chunk = tickers[i : i + CHUNK_SIZE]
    print(f"Pulling {i + 1} to {min(i + CHUNK_SIZE, len(tickers))} of {len(tickers)}...")
    try:
        df = blp.bdh(tickers=chunk, flds=FIELDS, start_date="2018-01-01", end_date="2026-03-20")
        # df has MultiIndex columns: (ticker, field)
        # Reshape to long format
        df.columns.names = ["ticker", "field"]
        long = df.stack(level=0).reset_index()
        long.columns = ["date", "ticker", "open", "high", "low", "close", "volume"]
        all_chunks.append(long)
        print(f"  Got {len(long)} rows")
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"  Error on chunk {i}: {e}")

print("Combining all chunks...")
result = pd.concat(all_chunks, ignore_index=True)
result["date"] = pd.to_datetime(result["date"])
result = result.sort_values(["ticker", "date"]).reset_index(drop=True)

out_path = os.path.join(os.path.dirname(__file__), "..", "data", "bloomberg", "sp500_ohlcv.csv")
os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(out_path), exist_ok=True)
result.to_csv(out_path, index=False)

print(f"\nDone! Saved {len(result):,} rows x {len(result.columns)} columns")
print(f"Tickers: {result['ticker'].nunique()}")
print(f"Date range: {result['date'].min()} to {result['date'].max()}")
print("\nSample:")
print(result.head(10))

file.content:
"""
#14 - Liquidity Metrics (critical for execution)
BQL: get(avg_volume_30d, bid_ask_spread, turnover) for(members('SPX Index'))
     with(dates=range(2015-01-01, 2026-03-17), fill=prev)
"""

import os

import pandas as pd
from xbbg import blp

print("Getting S&P 500 members...")
members = blp.bds("SPX Index", "INDX_MWEIGHT")
tickers = [t + " Equity" for t in members["member_ticker_and_exchange_code"].tolist()]
print(f"Found {len(tickers)} tickers")

FIELDS = ["VOLUME_AVG_30D", "BID_ASK_SPREAD", "TURNOVER"]
CHUNK_SIZE = 30
all_chunks = []

for i in range(0, len(tickers), CHUNK_SIZE):
    chunk = tickers[i : i + CHUNK_SIZE]
    print(f"Pulling {i + 1} to {min(i + CHUNK_SIZE, len(tickers))} of {len(tickers)}...")
    try:
        df = blp.bdh(
            tickers=chunk, flds=FIELDS, start_date="2015-01-01", end_date="2026-03-20", Fill="P"
        )
        df.columns.names = ["ticker", "field"]
        long = df.stack(level=0).reset_index()
        long.columns = ["date", "ticker", "avg_vol_30d", "bid_ask_spread", "turnover"]
        long["ticker"] = long["ticker"].str.replace(" Equity", "")
        all_chunks.append(long)
        print(f"  Got {len(long)} rows")
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"  Error on chunk {i}: {e}")

print("Combining all chunks...")
result = pd.concat(all_chunks, ignore_index=True)
result["date"] = pd.to_datetime(result["date"])
result = result.sort_values(["ticker", "date"]).reset_index(drop=True)

out_path = os.path.join(os.path.dirname(__file__), "..", "data", "bloomberg", "sp500_liquidity.csv")
os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(out_path), exist_ok=True)
result.to_csv(out_path, index=False)

print(f"\nDone! Saved {len(result):,} rows")
print(f"Tickers: {result['ticker'].nunique()}")

content.content:
# CLAUDE.md — Session orientation for Smart Wheel Engine

You are working on a probabilistic expected-value (EV) decision engine for
wheel strategies (short cash-secured puts → covered calls) on S&P 500 names.
This file is the structural contract: how the product thinks. Read it in full.

For machine/environment rehydration (cloning, Theta Terminal, regenerating
local data), see `docs/LAPTOP_SETUP.md`. For everything else, the pointer
block at the bottom routes you to the right on-demand document.

---

## 1. Mental model — four layers

1. **Data layer** (`data/`, `data_processed/`, `scripts/pull_*.py`) — OHLCV,
   option chains, IV, fundamentals, macro, news. Two providers, selected by
   `SWE_DATA_PROVIDER` (default `bloomberg`). Full capability matrix in
   `docs/DATA_POLICY.md` §2.
2. **Quant layer** (`engine/`) — Black-Scholes-Merton pricing + Greeks to
   3rd order, empirical forward distributions (non-overlapping → block
   bootstrap → HAR-RV cascade), POT-GPD tail risk, 4-state Gaussian HMM
   regime, Nelson-Siegel skew, Student-t copula CVaR, dealer GEX / walls /
   gamma-flip. SVI surface tooling exists but is dormant — see
   `DECISIONS.md` D9.
3. **Decision layer — the authoritative ranker.**
   - `engine/ev_engine.py` — `EVEngine.evaluate`. Runs event lockout →
     forward distribution → cost model → regime & dealer multipliers →
     returns `EVResult`.
   - `engine/wheel_runner.py` — `WheelRunner.rank_candidates_by_ev`. The
     one ranker every tradeable path routes through.
   - `engine/candidate_dossier.py` — `EnginePhaseReviewer` applies the
     downgrade rules R1–R10 (see §2).
4. **Interface layer** — `engine_api.py` (HTTP on `:8787`), `dashboard/`
   (Next.js), `engine/tradingview_bridge.py` (chart providers — sanity
   check, not a decider), `advisors/` (Buffett / Munger / Simons / Taleb
   committee, advisory only), local Ollama for memos.

---

## 2. The hard invariant — never break this

**No tradeable candidate bypasses `EVEngine.evaluate`.**

Chart providers, news sentiment, the advisor committee, the dealer
positioning overlay, and the TradingView bridge can all **downgrade** a
verdict (negative → blocked, proceed → review, proceed → skip). None of
them can **rescue** a negative-EV trade. The dealer multiplier
(`dealer_regime_multiplier` in `engine/dealer_positioning.py`) is clamped
to `[0.70, 1.05]` and only scales the final `ev_dollars` — it never
touches `ev_raw`.

If you add a new input (new data source, new advisor, a TradingView MCP
chart feed), wire it as a participant in a chained provider or as a
downgrade-only reviewer. Do not introduce a code path that converts a
non-tradeable candidate into a tradeable one without a fresh
`EVEngine.evaluate` call.

The `EnginePhaseReviewer` rules, for reference:

- R1: negative OR non-finite EV → blocked (hard stop; **R1a** at
  `engine/candidate_dossier.py` guards `+inf` / `-inf` / `NaN` via
  `math.isfinite` *before* the negative check, returning
  `verdict_reason="ev_non_finite"` distinct from `"negative_ev"` so the
  audit trail tells an unparseable engine value apart from an evaluated
  loss — see PR #204)
- R2: chart missing → review
- R3: spot mismatch > 2% → skip
- R4: phase contradiction → skip *(conditional/reserved — the rule is
  implemented and unit-tested but dormant in the production path: no
  current chart provider populates `visible_indicators['phase']` (empty
  through M1) and the ranker emits no `phase` on `ev_row`. It fires only
  when a phase-aware chart provider lands — see
  `docs/TRADINGVIEW_INTEGRATION.md`. Not a live downgrade today.)*
- R5: EV above threshold → proceed (below → review)
- R6: short-gamma regime + strike at/above put wall, or dealer regime
  near gamma flip → downgrade to review
- R7: *(D17 soft-warn — conditional on attached `PortfolioContext`)*
  portfolio VaR_95 (30-day horizon) above `max_var_pct × NAV` (default
  5%) → downgrade proceed → review. Skips silently when no context is
  attached OR when `check_var` lacks correlation/returns data — soft-warns
  don't fire on absent evidence.
- R8: *(D17 soft-warn — conditional on attached `PortfolioContext`,
  two trigger conditions mirroring R6.)* Either the C4 vol-spike stress
  drawdown > 8% NAV OR the candidate's underlying is in
  `short_gamma_amplifying` regime → downgrade proceed → review. Distinct
  `verdict_reason` per trigger.
- R9: *(D17 soft-warn — conditional on attached `PortfolioContext`,
  added in B2 closure.)* Sector concentration cap. If opening the
  candidate would push its GICS sector over `max_sector_pct × NAV`
  (default 25% per the D17 sector cap; same gate
  `engine.portfolio_risk_gates.check_sector_cap` the tracker applies
  as a HARD refusal at `open_short_put` time when
  `require_ev_authority=True`), downgrade proceed → review with
  `verdict_reason="sector_cap_breach"`. Skips silently when no
  context is attached OR `nav == 0` — soft-warns don't fire on
  absent evidence.
- R10: *(D17 soft-warn — conditional on attached `PortfolioContext`,
  F4 damage-bounding addition.)* Single-name (per-underlying)
  exposure cap. Sits BENEATH R9: even when sector cap is satisfied,
  a single ticker concentrated as the only name in its sector could
  exceed the per-name floor. If opening the candidate would push
  the SINGLE-NAME short-option notional over `max_single_name_pct ×
  NAV` (default 10% per `engine.portfolio_risk_gates.check_single_name_cap`;
  same gate the tracker applies as a HARD refusal at
  `open_short_put` time when `require_ev_authority=True`),
  downgrade proceed → review with `verdict_reason="single_name_breach"`.
  Bounds F4-style idiosyncratic-drawdown damage that no market-wide
  regime detector can predict (see `docs/F4_TAIL_RISK_DIAGNOSTIC.md`
  §10). Same Q3 missing-data semantics as R7-R9.

---

## 3. NEVER — out of scope by design (get explicit consent first)

- **Never bypass `EVEngine.evaluate`.** No code path converts a
  non-tradeable candidate into a tradeable one without a fresh evaluate
  call (§2).
- **Never weaken the downgrade-only reviewer contract.** Reviewers
  downgrade; they never upgrade.
- **Never alter the dealer multiplier clamp `[0.70, 1.05]`** — asymmetric
  by design.
- **No broker / OMS / order routing surface.** The engine produces ranked
  candidates and memos.
- **No tick-level order flow / sub-minute features.** Theta v3 does not
  expose realtime stock quotes at this tier.
- **No non-S&P-500 universe and no non-wheel strategies.** Short puts +
  covered calls + timing-gated strangles only.
- **Branch + PR for every change.** Never commit to `main` directly.

---

## 4. Fresh-session bring-up

1. Confirm the provider. Default is `bloomberg`; verify with:

   ```python
   from engine.wheel_runner import WheelRunner
   print(type(WheelRunner().connector).__name__)
   ```

   Expect `MarketDataConnector` in a Cowork sandbox. **Always log which
   provider was actually selected** — silent provider selection is a
   recurring bug source.

2. Sanity-check the data layer with the 5-ticker smoke test:

   ```python
   from engine.wheel_runner import WheelRunner
   df = WheelRunner().rank_candidates_by_ev(
       tickers=["AAPL", "MSFT", "JPM", "XOM", "UNH"],
       top_n=10, min_ev_dollars=-1e9,
       include_diagnostic_fields=True,
   )
   ```

   Runs in ~2 s. Five rows with non-null `ev_dollars`, `iv`, and `premium`
   means the Bloomberg CSVs + connector + EV engine path is healthy.

3. For any change touching the decision layer (`ev_engine.py`,
   `wheel_runner.py`, `candidate_dossier.py`): run the full test suite
   (`pytest tests/ -v`), not just the targeted file. Invariants are
   cross-cutting.

For sandbox-vs-laptop capability differences (what works in Cowork vs.
needs Theta Terminal), see `docs/DATA_POLICY.md` §7.

---

## 5. Where to look next

| For… | Read |
|---|---|
| Onboarding contract for any AI agent | `AGENTS.md` |
| What is authoritative / WIP / deprecated right now | `PROJECT_STATE.md` |
| Per-module purpose + decision-layer role | `MODULE_INDEX.md` |
| Test taxonomy + launch-blocker subset | `TESTING.md` |
| Every tracked file by purpose — grep, don't read | `FILE_MANIFEST.md` |
| Data layer (tiers, provider matrix, refresh, sandbox caveats) | `docs/DATA_POLICY.md` |
| Launch-blocker invariants before merging | `docs/LAUNCH_READINESS.md` |
| Greek units (canonical) | `docs/GREEKS_UNIT_CONTRACT.md` |
| TradingView wiring (engine bridge + analyst workspace) | `docs/TRADINGVIEW_INTEGRATION.md` |
| Commit / PR format | `COMMIT_GUIDE.md` |
| *Why* a structural choice was made | `DECISIONS.md` |

1. Both scripts use `df.stack(level=0)` — assuming **pandas MultiIndex columns**. But xbbg 1.2.5 returns **narwhals**. I must verify what `blp.bdh`/`blp.bds` actually return now.
2. `pull_liquidity.py` outputs `date,ticker,avg_vol_30d,bid_ask_spread,turnover` — but the committed schema is `date,avg_vol_30d,turnover,shares_out,ticker`. I must fix it (add `EQY_SH_OUT`→`shares_out`, drop `bid_ask_spread`, reorder).

Let me run the **tiny frugal bdh/bds test** (2 tickers, ~5 days) and read the BQL doc + smoke test in parallel.

1	# Bloomberg BQL pulls — copy/paste into Excel
2	
3	One section per missing dataset. For each:
4	1. Paste the BQL into cell A1 of a new sheet.
5	2. Wait for the formula to resolve (usually < 60s for S&P-wide queries).
6	3. Save the sheet as `<filename>.csv` under `data/bloomberg/`.
7	4. Re-run `python scripts/feature_smoke_test.py` — the new data will show up in its own section.
8	
9	All queries use the BQL functional form `=BQL.QUERY("…")`. Column aliases
10	in the queries match what the loader expects (see `data/consolidated_loader.py`
11	column maps).
12	
13	> **Note on date range.** Queries are written against `range(2015-01-01, 2026-04-23)`.
14	> Adjust the end date to *today* before running if you want the freshest data.
15	
16	---
17	
18	## 1. Macro / economic-release calendar  → `sp500_macro_calendar.csv`
19	
20	Activates `engine/event_gate.py` macro-buffer path. Today there's no macro
21	calendar at all; FOMC, CPI, NFP events are invisible to the gate.
22	
23	```bql
24	=BQL.QUERY("get(eco_release_dt, eco_release_event, eco_importance, eco_country) for(['FDTR Index', 'CPI YOY Index', 'NFP TCH Index', 'GDP CQOQ Index', 'NAPMPMI Index', 'INJCJC Index', 'FOMC Dec Index', 'RSTAMOM Index', 'CONCCONF Index', 'INDPRO Index']) with(dates=range(2015-01-01, 2026-12-31))")
25	```
26	
27	Expected columns:
28	`release_date, event, importance, country, ticker`
29	
30	---
31	
32	## 2. Corporate actions (splits, specials, M&A)  → `sp500_corporate_actions.csv`
33	
34	Currently the file on disk is 2 bytes. Required by the event gate to avoid
35	early-assignment on short calls around specials / splits.
36	
37	```bql
38	=BQL.QUERY("get(cac_announcement_date, cac_effective_date, cac_type, cac_ratio, cac_amount) for(members('SPX Index')) with(dates=range(2015-01-01, 2026-12-31))")
39	```
40	
41	Expected columns:
42	`announcement_date, effective_date, action_type, ratio, amount, ticker`
43	
44	---
45	
46	## 3. Short interest & borrow fee  → `sp500_short_interest.csv`
47	
48	`engine/risk_manager.py` sizes positions without knowing which names are
49	heavily shorted. Crowded-short names blow up put sellers.
50	
51	```bql
52	=BQL.QUERY("get(eqy_short_interest, eqy_short_interest_pct_of_float, equity_short_borrow_rate_net, eqy_sh_out, eqy_float_pct) for(members('SPX Index')) with(dates=range(2020-01-01, 2026-12-31), fill=prev)")
53	```
54	
55	Expected columns:
56	`date, ticker, short_interest, short_interest_pct_float, borrow_rate_net, shares_out, float_pct`
57	
58	---
59	
60	## 4. VIX futures term structure UX1-UX7  → `vix_futures_curve.csv`
61	
62	You already have VIX3M/VIX6M. Full UX1-UX7 lets the regime detector see
63	contango vs backwardation precisely.
64	
65	```bql
66	=BQL.QUERY("get(px_last) for(['UX1 Index', 'UX2 Index', 'UX3 Index', 'UX4 Index', 'UX5 Index', 'UX6 Index', 'UX7 Index']) with(dates=range(2015-01-01, 2026-12-31), fill=prev)")
67	```
68	
69	Expected columns:
70	`date, ticker (UX1..UX7), close`
71	
72	---
73	
74	## 5. Skew / vol-of-vol indices  → `vol_indices.csv`
75	
76	SKEW captures tail-risk regimes VIX misses. VVIX captures vol-of-vol
77	(important for dealer-positioning module). Single-pull, one tidy file.
78	
79	```bql
80	=BQL.QUERY("get(px_last) for(['SKEW Index', 'VVIX Index', 'VIX9D Index', 'VXN Index', 'RVX Index', 'VXEEM Index', 'OVX Index', 'GVZ Index']) with(dates=range(2015-01-01, 2026-12-31), fill=prev)")
81	```
82	
83	Expected columns:
84	`date, ticker, close`
85	
86	---
87	
88	## 6. Analyst revisions stream (not snapshot)  → `sp500_analyst_revisions.csv`
89	
90	`sp500_analyst.csv` is one row per ticker today. Revision momentum is the
91	clean fundamentals signal — monotonic on short-put win rate.
92	
93	```bql
94	=BQL.QUERY("get(best_eps, best_target_price, best_analyst_rating, tot_analyst_rec) for(members('SPX Index')) with(dates=range(2015-01-01, 2026-12-31), fill=prev)")
95	```
96	
97	Expected columns:
98	`date, ticker, best_eps, best_target_price, best_analyst_rating, tot_analyst_rec`
99	
100	---
101	
102	## 7. Realised-correlation index  → `spx_correlation.csv`
103	
104	Correlation regime is first-order for portfolio CVaR. Cheap pull.
105	
106	```bql
107	=BQL.QUERY("get(px_last) for(['COR3M Index', 'COR1M Index', 'COR6M Index']) with(dates=range(2015-01-01, 2026-12-31), fill=prev)")
108	```
109	
110	---
111	
112	## 8. Issuer credit spreads (CDS 5y)  → `sp500_cds.csv`
113	
114	Complements the `altman_z_score` that's already in `sp500_credit_risk.csv`.
115	Live CDS reprices faster than the Altman Z.
116	
117	```bql
118	=BQL.QUERY("get(cds_spread_5y) for(members('SPX Index')) with(dates=range(2018-01-01, 2026-12-31), fill=prev)")
119	```
120	
121	---
122	
123	## 9. Intraday SPX option market-maker hedge proxy  → `spx_mm_hedge.csv`
124	
125	Dealer-positioning module today estimates GEX from single-ticker OI.
126	SPX-level MM hedge proxy is what institutional desks use.
127	
128	```bql
129	=BQL.QUERY("get(dealer_gex_total, dealer_dex_total, dealer_gex_profile) for(['SPX Index', 'SPY US Equity', 'QQQ US Equity']) with(dates=range(2020-01-01, 2026-12-31), fill=prev)")
130	```
131	
132	Note: this one requires a BQL dealer-positioning subscription. If your
133	tier doesn't expose it, skip and rely on `engine/dealer_positioning.py`'s
134	approximation from option chains.
135	
136	---
137	
138	## 10. ETF sector constituent weights (rebalancing flow signal)  → `sector_etf_constituents.csv`
139	
140	```bql
141	=BQL.QUERY("get(weight) for(members('XLF US Equity') + members('XLK US Equity') + members('XLV US Equity') + members('XLY US Equity') + members('XLP US Equity') + members('XLE US Equity') + members('XLI US Equity') + members('XLU US Equity') + members('XLB US Equity') + members('XLC US Equity') + members('XLRE US Equity')) with(dates=range(2018-01-01, 2026-12-31, frq=M), fill=prev)")
142	```
143	
144	---
145	
146	## 11. Foreign-index vol (for global regime)  → `global_vol_indices.csv`
147	
148	```bql
149	=BQL.QUERY("get(px_last) for(['V2X Index', 'VHSI Index', 'VNKY Index', 'VKOSPI Index']) with(dates=range(2015-01-01, 2026-12-31), fill=prev)")
150	```
151	
152	---
153	
154	## 12. Rates vol & FX vol (macro regime)  → `rates_fx_vol.csv`
155	
156	```bql
157	=BQL.QUERY("get(px_last) for(['MOVE Index', 'CVIX Index', 'JPMVXYG7 Index']) with(dates=range(2015-01-01, 2026-12-31), fill=prev)")
158	```
159	
160	---
161	
162	# After you've pulled everything
163	
164	1. Save each CSV to `data/bloomberg/`.
165	2. Re-run the feature-pipeline backfill to let the new fields propagate:
166	   ```
167	   python scripts/backfill_features.py --force --workers 6
168	   ```
169	3. Run the smoke test to validate:
170	   ```
171	   python scripts/feature_smoke_test.py
172	   ```
173	4. Wire the new files into `data/consolidated_loader.py` (one `load_*`
174	   method per file, following the existing pattern of `load_analyst()`).
175

in the queries match what the loader expects (see `data/consolidated_loader.py`
column maps).

> **Note on date range.** Queries are written against `range(2015-01-01, 2026-04-23)`.
> Adjust the end date to *today* before running if you want the freshest data.

---

## 1. Macro / economic-release calendar  → `sp500_macro_calendar.csv`

Activates `engine/event_gate.py` macro-buffer path. Today there's no macro
calendar at all; FOMC, CPI, NFP events are invisible to the gate.

```bql
=BQL.QUERY("get(eco_release_dt, eco_release_event, eco_importance, eco_country) for(['FDTR Index', 'CPI YOY Index', 'NFP TCH Index', 'GDP CQOQ Index', 'NAPMPMI Index', 'INJCJC Index', 'FOMC Dec Index', 'RSTAMOM Index', 'CONCCONF Index', 'INDPRO Index']) with(dates=range(2015-01-01, 2026-12-31))")
```

Expected columns:
`release_date, event, importance, country, ticker`

---

## 2. Corporate actions (splits, specials, M&A)  → `sp500_corporate_actions.csv`

Currently the file on disk is 2 bytes. Required by the event gate to avoid
early-assignment on short calls around specials / splits.

```bql
=BQL.QUERY("get(cac_announcement_date, cac_effective_date, cac_type, cac_ratio, cac_amount) for(members('SPX Index')) with(dates=range(2015-01-01, 2026-12-31))")
```

Expected columns:
`announcement_date, effective_date, action_type, ratio, amount, ticker`

---

## 3. Short interest & borrow fee  → `sp500_short_interest.csv`

`engine/risk_manager.py` sizes positions without knowing which names are
heavily shorted. Crowded-short names blow up put sellers.

```bql
=BQL.QUERY("get(eqy_short_interest, eqy_short_interest_pct_of_float, equity_short_borrow_rate_net, eqy_sh_out, eqy_float_pct) for(members('SPX Index')) with(dates=range(2020-01-01, 2026-12-31), fill=prev)")
```

Expected columns:
`date, ticker, short_interest, short_interest_pct_float, borrow_rate_net, shares_out, float_pct`

---

## 4. VIX futures term structure UX1-UX7  → `vix_futures_curve.csv`

You already have VIX3M/VIX6M. Full UX1-UX7 lets the regime detector see
contango vs backwardation precisely.

```bql

```

Expected columns:

---

## 5. Skew / vol-of-vol indices  → `vol_indices.csv`

SKEW captures tail-risk regimes VIX misses. VVIX captures vol-of-vol
(important for dealer-positioning module). Single-pull, one tidy file.

```bql
=BQL.QUERY("get(px_last) for(['SKEW Index', 'VVIX Index', 'VIX9D Index', 'VXN Index', 'RVX Index', 'VXEEM Index', 'OVX Index', 'GVZ Index']) with(dates=range(2015-01-01, 2026-12-31), fill=prev)")
```

Expected columns:
`date, ticker, close`

---

## 6. Analyst revisions stream (not snapshot)  → `sp500_analyst_revisions.csv`

`sp500_analyst.csv` is one row per ticker today. Revision momentum is the
clean fundamentals signal — monotonic on short-put win rate.

```bql

```

Expected columns:
`date, ticker, best_eps, best_target_price, best_analyst_rating, tot_analyst_rec`

---

## 7. Realised-correlation index  → `spx_correlation.csv`

Correlation regime is first-order for portfolio CVaR. Cheap pull.

```bql
=BQL.QUERY("get(px_last) for(['COR3M Index', 'COR1M Index', 'COR6M Index']) with(dates=range(2015-01-01, 2026-12-31), fill=prev)")
```

---

## 8. Issuer credit spreads (CDS 5y)  → `sp500_cds.csv`

Complements the `altman_z_score` that's already in `sp500_credit_risk.csv`.
Live CDS reprices faster than the Altman Z.

```bql
=BQL.QUERY("get(cds_spread_5y) for(members('SPX Index')) with(dates=range(2018-01-01, 2026-12-31), fill=prev)")
```

---

## 9. Intraday SPX option market-maker hedge proxy  → `spx_mm_hedge.csv`

Dealer-positioning module today estimates GEX from single-ticker OI.
SPX-level MM hedge proxy is what institutional desks use.

```bql
=BQL.QUERY("get(dealer_gex_total, dealer_dex_total, dealer_gex_profile) for(['SPX Index', 'SPY US Equity', 'QQQ US Equity']) with(dates=range(2020-01-01, 2026-12-31), fill=prev)")
```

Note: this one requires a BQL dealer-positioning subscription. If your
tier doesn't expose it, skip and rely on `engine/dealer_positioning.py`'s
approximation from option chains.

---

## 10. ETF sector constituent weights (rebalancing flow signal)  → `sector_etf_constituents.csv`

```bql
=BQL.QUERY("get(weight) for(members('XLF US Equity') + members('XLK US Equity') + members('XLV US Equity') + members('XLY US Equity') + members('XLP US Equity') + members('XLE US Equity') + members('XLI US Equity') + members('XLU US Equity') + members('XLB US Equity') + members('XLC US Equity') + members('XLRE US Equity')) with(dates=range(2018-01-01, 2026-12-31, frq=M), fill=prev)")
```

---

## 11. Foreign-index vol (for global regime)  → `global_vol_indices.csv`

```bql
=BQL.QUERY("get(px_last) for(['V2X Index', 'VHSI Index', 'VNKY Index', 'VKOSPI Index']) with(dates=range(2015-01-01, 2026-12-31), fill=prev)")
```

---

## 12. Rates vol & FX vol (macro regime)  → `rates_fx_vol.csv`

```bql
=BQL.QUERY("get(px_last) for(['MOVE Index', 'CVIX Index', 'JPMVXYG7 Index']) with(dates=range(2015-01-01, 2026-12-31), fill=prev)")
```

---

# After you've pulled everything

1. Save each CSV to `data/bloomberg/`.
2. Re-run the feature-pipeline backfill to let the new fields propagate:
   ```
   python scripts/backfill_features.py --force --workers 6
   ```
3. Run the smoke test to validate:
   ```
   python scripts/feature_smoke_test.py
   ```
4. Wire the new files into `data/consolidated_loader.py` (one `load_*`
   method per file, following the existing pattern of `load_analyst()`).

input.command:
$py = "C:\Users\mertmert\Desktop\bbg-venv\Scripts\python.exe"
$env:PYTHONUTF8 = "1"; $env:PYTHONIOENCODING = "utf-8"
$test = @'
import os, pandas as pd
base = r"C:\Users\mertmert\Desktop\smart-wheel-engine\data\bloomberg"
files = ["sp500_ohlcv.csv","sp500_liquidity.csv","sp500_vol_iv_full.csv","sp500_corporate_actions.csv",
         "sp500_vix_full.csv","vix_term_structure.csv","treasury_yields.csv","sp500_macro.csv","sp500_iv_history.csv"]
for f in files:
    p = os.path.join(base,f)
    if not os.path.exists(p):
        print(f"\n##### {f}: MISSING"); continue
    sz = os.path.getsize(p)
    print(f"\n##### {f}  ({sz:,} bytes)")
    if sz < 5:
        with open(p) as fh: print("  content:", repr(fh.read())); continue
    # read header only
    with open(p, encoding="utf-8", errors="replace") as fh:
        hdr = fh.readline().strip()
    print("  HEADER:", hdr)
    # For big files, read just date/ticker columns if present
    cols = hdr.split(",")
    try:
        usecols = [c for c in cols if c.lower() in ("date","ticker")]
        if usecols:
            d = pd.read_csv(p, usecols=usecols)
            print("  rows:", f"{len(d):,}")
            if "date" in d.columns:
                print("  date min/max:", str(d['date'].min()), "->", str(d['date'].max()))
            if "ticker" in d.columns:
                tk = d['ticker'].dropna().unique()
                print("  n_tickers:", len(tk), " sample:", list(tk[:5]))
        else:
            d = pd.read_csv(p, nrows=3)
            print("  rows(sampled head only); first 3:\n", d.to_string())
    except Exception as e:
        print("  read err:", e)
'@
$test | & $py -

<system-reminder>[Truncated: PARTIAL view — showing lines 1-1413 of 1792 total (26941 tokens, cap 25000). Call Read with offset=1414 limit=1413 for the next page, or Grep to find a specific section. Do NOT answer from this page alone if the answer may be further in the file.]</system-reminder>

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""
3	Smart Wheel Engine — Feature Smoke Test Harness
4	================================================
5	
6	Professional-grade end-to-end smoke test. Exercises every user-facing feature
7	of the product in one run and reports a structured PASS / FAIL / SKIP table.
8	
9	Use this whenever you want a fast answer to: "is my data pipeline working?",
10	"is the brain (EV engine) wired up?", "does the dashboard API respond?".
11	
12	Exit codes:
13	  0 — every check passed or was explicitly skipped
14	  1 — one or more checks failed
15	
16	Usage::
17	
18	    python scripts/feature_smoke_test.py                # run everything
19	    python scripts/feature_smoke_test.py --fast         # skip network/slow paths
20	    python scripts/feature_smoke_test.py --section ev   # run one section
21	    python scripts/feature_smoke_test.py --json > out.json   # machine-readable
22	    python scripts/feature_smoke_test.py --verbose      # show all details
23	
24	Design notes:
25	  * No external test framework. Single runnable script.
26	  * Each check is a tiny closure returning either a short detail string, None,
27	    or raising either ``Skip(reason)`` (not a failure) or any Exception (FAIL).
28	  * Live-data paths (Theta, Bloomberg, HTTP API) auto-skip when not reachable.
29	  * No mocking of the brain: EV engine, risk manager, wheel runner all run
30	    against synthetic but realistic inputs so real bugs surface.
31	"""
32	
33	from __future__ import annotations
34	
35	import argparse
36	import json
37	import os
38	import sys
39	import time
40	import traceback
41	from collections.abc import Callable
42	from dataclasses import asdict, dataclass
43	from pathlib import Path
44	from typing import Any
45	
46	_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
47	# Insert project root at front unconditionally — the "not in sys.path" guard
48	# is unreliable on Windows where normalised and unnormalised path strings can
49	# both be present and both miss string equality against the repo root.
50	sys.path.insert(0, str(_ROOT))
51	os.environ.setdefault("PYTHONPATH", str(_ROOT))
52	
53	import numpy as np  # noqa: E402
54	import pandas as pd  # noqa: E402
55	
56	# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
57	# Framework
58	# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
59	PASS = "PASS"
60	FAIL = "FAIL"
61	SKIP = "SKIP"
62	
63	
64	class Skip(Exception):
65	    """Raise inside a check to mark it as SKIP rather than FAIL."""
66	
67	
68	@dataclass
69	class CheckResult:
70	    section: str
71	    name: str
72	    status: str
73	    detail: str = ""
74	    duration_ms: float = 0.0
75	
76	
77	class Harness:
78	    def __init__(self, section_filter: str | None = None, fast: bool = False) -> None:
79	        self.section_filter = section_filter
80	        self.fast = fast
81	        self.results: list[CheckResult] = []
82	        self._current_section = ""
83	
84	    def section(self, name: str) -> None:
85	        self._current_section = name
86	
87	    def run(self, name: str, fn: Callable[[], Any]) -> None:
88	        if self.section_filter and self.section_filter.lower() not in self._current_section.lower():
89	            return
90	        t0 = time.perf_counter()
91	        try:
92	            detail = fn() or ""
93	            status = PASS
94	        except Skip as s:
95	            detail = str(s)
96	            status = SKIP
97	        except AssertionError as e:
98	            status = FAIL
99	            detail = f"assertion: {e}"
100	        except Exception as e:  # noqa: BLE001 — smoke harness intentionally catches all
101	            status = FAIL
102	            detail = f"{type(e).__name__}: {e}"
103	        elapsed = (time.perf_counter() - t0) * 1000.0
104	        self.results.append(CheckResult(self._current_section, name, status, str(detail), elapsed))
105	
106	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
107	    def report(self, verbose: bool, as_json: bool) -> int:
108	        if as_json:
109	            print(json.dumps([asdict(r) for r in self.results], indent=2))
110	            return 0 if not any(r.status == FAIL for r in self.results) else 1
111	
112	        # Human report.
113	        sections: dict[str, list[CheckResult]] = {}
114	        for r in self.results:
115	            sections.setdefault(r.section, []).append(r)
116	
117	        total_pass = sum(1 for r in self.results if r.status == PASS)
118	        total_fail = sum(1 for r in self.results if r.status == FAIL)
119	        total_skip = sum(1 for r in self.results if r.status == SKIP)
120	
121	        def color(s: str, status: str) -> str:
122	            if not sys.stdout.isatty() or os.environ.get("NO_COLOR"):
123	                return s
124	            code = {"PASS": "\033[32m", "FAIL": "\033[31m", "SKIP": "\033[33m"}.get(status, "")
125	            return f"{code}{s}\033[0m" if code else s
126	
127	        print()
128	        print("=" * 78)
129	        print(" Smart Wheel Engine — Feature Smoke Test")
130	        print("=" * 78)
131	
132	        for sect, items in sections.items():
133	            p = sum(1 for r in items if r.status == PASS)
134	            f = sum(1 for r in items if r.status == FAIL)
135	            s = sum(1 for r in items if r.status == SKIP)
136	            header = f" [{sect}]  {p} pass  {f} fail  {s} skip"
137	            print()
138	            print(header)
139	            print("-" * len(header))
140	            for r in items:
141	                tag = color(f"{r.status:<4}", r.status)
142	                dur = f"{r.duration_ms:>6.1f}ms"
143	                line = f"  {tag}  {dur}  {r.name}"
144	                if r.status == FAIL or (verbose and r.detail):
145	                    line += f"    — {r.detail}"
146	                print(line)
147	
148	        print()
149	        print("=" * 78)
150	        summary = (
151	            f" Total: {len(self.results)}   "
152	            f"{color(f'{total_pass} PASS', PASS)}   "
153	            f"{color(f'{total_fail} FAIL', FAIL)}   "
154	            f"{color(f'{total_skip} SKIP', SKIP)}"
155	        )
156	        print(summary)
157	        print("=" * 78)
158	        return 0 if total_fail == 0 else 1
159	
160	
161	# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
162	# Shared fixtures (cheap; built once, reused across checks)
163	# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
164	def _synth_ohlcv(n_days: int = 1500, seed: int = 7) -> pd.DataFrame:
165	    """Deterministic synthetic OHLCV series — long enough for realized-vol
166	    estimators and the 504-day HMM window used by the regime detector."""
167	    rng = np.random.default_rng(seed)
168	    idx = pd.date_range("2022-01-03", periods=n_days, freq="B")
169	    log_rets = rng.normal(0.0003, 0.012, n_days)
170	    close = 100.0 * np.exp(np.cumsum(log_rets))
171	    # O/H/L with ~40bp intraday noise around close.
172	    openp = close * (1 + rng.normal(0, 0.004, n_days))
173	    high = np.maximum.reduce([openp, close]) * (1 + np.abs(rng.normal(0, 0.003, n_days)))
174	    low = np.minimum.reduce([openp, close]) * (1 - np.abs(rng.normal(0, 0.003, n_days)))
175	    vol = rng.integers(1_000_000, 5_000_000, n_days)
176	    return pd.DataFrame(
177	        {"open": openp, "high": high, "low": low, "close": close, "volume": vol},
178	        index=idx,
179	    )
180	
181	
182	def _synth_chain(spot: float = 100.0, dte_days: int = 32) -> pd.DataFrame:
183	    """Synthetic options chain with clean bid/ask around a BSM mid."""
184	    from datetime import date, timedelta
185	
186	    from engine.option_pricer import black_scholes_price
187	
188	    expiry = date.today() + timedelta(days=dte_days)
189	    T = dte_days / 365.0
190	    iv = 0.25
191	    strikes = np.arange(spot * 0.80, spot * 1.201, 2.5)
192	    rows = []
193	    for K in strikes:
194	        for opt, right in (("put", "P"), ("call", "C")):
195	            mid = black_scholes_price(spot, K, T, 0.05, iv, opt, 0.0)
196	            mid = max(mid, 0.05)
197	            rows.append(
198	                {
199	                    "strike": float(K),
200	                    "option_type": right,
201	                    "right": opt,
202	                    "expiration": expiry,
203	                    "bid": round(mid * 0.97, 2),
204	                    "ask": round(mid * 1.03, 2),
205	                    "implied_vol": iv,
206	                    "iv": iv,
207	                    "open_interest": 500,
208	                    "volume": 100,
209	                    "delta": (
210	                        -0.5
211	                        if opt == "put" and K > spot
212	                        else 0.5
213	                        if opt == "call" and K < spot
214	                        else 0.25
215	                        if opt == "put"
216	                        else -0.25
217	                    ),
218	                }
219	            )
220	    return pd.DataFrame(rows)
221	
222	
223	class _FakeConnector:
224	    """Minimal deterministic connector used by wheel-runner integration checks."""
225	
226	    def __init__(self, ticker: str = "TESTA", chain: pd.DataFrame | None = None) -> None:
227	        self._ticker = ticker
228	        self._ohlcv = _synth_ohlcv()
229	        self._chain = chain if chain is not None else _synth_chain()
230	
231	    def get_ohlcv(self, ticker, start_date=None, end_date=None):  # noqa: ARG002
232	        return self._ohlcv
233	
234	    def get_fundamentals(self, ticker):  # noqa: ARG002
235	        return {"implied_vol_atm": 0.25, "volatility_30d": 0.22, "dividend_yield": 0.005}
236	
237	    def get_risk_free_rate(self, as_of=None):  # noqa: ARG002
238	        return 0.05
239	
240	    def get_next_earnings(self, ticker, as_of=None):  # noqa: ARG002
241	        return None
242	
243	    def get_universe(self):
244	        return [self._ticker]
245	
246	    def get_options(self, ticker):  # noqa: ARG002
247	        return self._chain.copy()
248	
249	    def get_credit_risk(self, ticker):  # noqa: ARG002
250	        return None
251	
252	    def get_option_chain(self, ticker, dte_target=None):  # noqa: ARG002
253	        return self._chain.copy()
254	
255	
256	# ======================================================================
257	# CHECKS
258	# ======================================================================
259	def register_checks(h: Harness) -> None:
260	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
261	    # 1. Option pricing — the math spine
262	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
263	    h.section("01 option_pricer")
264	    from engine.option_pricer import (
265	        american_option_greeks,
266	        american_option_price,
267	        black_scholes_all_greeks,
268	        black_scholes_color,
269	        black_scholes_delta,
270	        black_scholes_gamma,
271	        black_scholes_price,
272	        black_scholes_rho,
273	        black_scholes_speed,
274	        black_scholes_theta,
275	        black_scholes_ultima,
276	        black_scholes_vega,
277	        implied_volatility,
278	        vectorized_bs_all_greeks,
279	    )
280	
281	    def bsm_call():
282	        p = black_scholes_price(100, 100, 30 / 365, 0.05, 0.25, "call", 0.0)
283	        assert 2.5 < p < 4.5, p
284	        return f"ATM call price={p:.3f}"
285	
286	    def bsm_put():
287	        p = black_scholes_price(100, 100, 30 / 365, 0.05, 0.25, "put", 0.0)
288	        assert 2.0 < p < 4.5, p
289	        return f"ATM put price={p:.3f}"
290	
291	    def bsm_put_call_parity():
292	        S, K, T, r, sigma, q = 100, 100, 30 / 365, 0.05, 0.25, 0.0
293	        c = black_scholes_price(S, K, T, r, sigma, "call", q)
294	        p = black_scholes_price(S, K, T, r, sigma, "put", q)
295	        lhs = c - p
296	        rhs = S * np.exp(-q * T) - K * np.exp(-r * T)
297	        err = abs(lhs - rhs)
298	        assert err < 1e-6, f"parity break {err:.2e}"
299	        return f"residual={err:.2e}"
300	
301	    def bsm_greeks_first_order():
302	        g = {
303	            "delta": black_scholes_delta(100, 100, 30 / 365, 0.05, 0.25, "call"),
304	            "gamma": black_scholes_gamma(100, 100, 30 / 365, 0.05, 0.25),
305	            "theta": black_scholes_theta(100, 100, 30 / 365, 0.05, 0.25, "call"),
306	            "vega": black_scholes_vega(100, 100, 30 / 365, 0.05, 0.25),
307	            "rho": black_scholes_rho(100, 100, 30 / 365, 0.05, 0.25, "call"),
308	        }
309	        assert 0.45 < g["delta"] < 0.60, g["delta"]
310	        assert g["gamma"] > 0, g["gamma"]
311	        return ", ".join(f"{k}={v:.4f}" for k, v in g.items())
312	
313	    def bsm_higher_order_greeks():
314	        d = black_scholes_all_greeks(100, 100, 30 / 365, 0.05, 0.25, "call", 0.0, True)
315	        # Verify core keys are present (second-order Greeks populated).
316	        for k in ("delta", "gamma", "theta", "vega"):
317	            assert k in d, k
318	        sp = black_scholes_speed(100, 100, 30 / 365, 0.05, 0.25)
319	        co = black_scholes_color(100, 100, 30 / 365, 0.05, 0.25)
320	        ul = black_scholes_ultima(100, 100, 30 / 365, 0.05, 0.25)
321	        assert all(np.isfinite([sp, co, ul]))
322	        return f"speed={sp:.6f} color={co:.6f} ultima={ul:.3f}"
323	
324	    def bsm_vectorized():
325	        Ks = np.array([90.0, 95.0, 100.0, 105.0, 110.0])
326	        out = vectorized_bs_all_greeks(100.0, Ks, 30 / 365, 0.05, 0.25, "call", 0.0)
327	        assert len(out["delta"]) == len(Ks)
328	        assert all(np.diff(out["delta"]) < 0)  # monotonic in K
329	        return f"n={len(Ks)} monotone delta OK"
330	
331	    def iv_solver():
332	        true_sigma = 0.32
333	        price = black_scholes_price(100, 105, 45 / 365, 0.05, true_sigma, "call", 0.01)
334	        iv = implied_volatility(price, 100, 105, 45 / 365, 0.05, "call", q=0.01)
335	        assert abs(iv - true_sigma) < 1e-3, f"iv={iv}"
336	        return f"recovered iv={iv:.5f} (true={true_sigma})"
337	
338	    def iv_solver_edge_itm():
339	        # Deep ITM should still converge via Brent fallback.
340	        true_sigma = 0.45
341	        price = black_scholes_price(100, 80, 60 / 365, 0.05, true_sigma, "call")
342	        iv = implied_volatility(price, 100, 80, 60 / 365, 0.05, "call")
343	        assert abs(iv - true_sigma) < 5e-3, iv
344	        return f"ITM recovery iv={iv:.4f}"
345	
346	    def american_baw_call_no_div():
347	        # Without dividends, American call should equal European call.
348	        euro = black_scholes_price(100, 100, 90 / 365, 0.05, 0.30, "call", 0.0)
349	        am = american_option_price(100, 100, 90 / 365, 0.05, 0.30, "call", 0.0)
350	        assert abs(am - euro) < 0.05, f"am={am} euro={euro}"
351	        return f"am={am:.4f} euro={euro:.4f}"
352	
353	    def american_baw_put_early_exercise_premium():
354	        euro = black_scholes_price(100, 100, 90 / 365, 0.05, 0.30, "put", 0.0)
355	        am = american_option_price(100, 100, 90 / 365, 0.05, 0.30, "put", 0.0)
356	        # American put >= European put (early-exercise premium).
357	        assert am >= euro - 1e-6, f"am={am} euro={euro}"
358	        return f"EEP = {am - euro:.4f}"
359	
360	    def american_greeks_path():
361	        g = american_option_greeks(100, 105, 60 / 365, 0.05, 0.28, "put")
362	        for k in ("delta", "gamma", "theta", "vega"):
363	            assert np.isfinite(g.get(k, float("nan"))), k
364	        return f"delta={g['delta']:.4f} gamma={g['gamma']:.5f}"
365	
366	    h.run("bsm_call_price", bsm_call)

457	    from engine.skew_dynamics import ivs_dislocation_score, skew_momentum, skew_slope
458	    from engine.volatility_surface import SVICalibrator, create_constant_surface
459	
460	    def svi_calibration():
461	        # Synthetic SVI-shaped smile. The calibrator expects IVs (not total
462	        # variance) plus a forward and time-to-expiry so it can build the
463	        # internal total-variance surface.
464	        spot = 100.0
465	        T = 60 / 365.0
466	        strikes = np.linspace(80.0, 120.0, 15)
467	        k = np.log(strikes / spot)
468	        ivs = 0.22 + 0.08 * k**2 - 0.03 * k
469	        try:
470	            params = SVICalibrator().calibrate(
471	                strikes=strikes,
472	                ivs=ivs,
473	                forward=spot,
474	                T=T,
475	            )
476	        except Exception as e:
477	            raise Skip(f"SVI unavailable: {e}") from e
478	        assert params is not None
479	        return "SVI fit converged"
480	
481	    def constant_surface():
482	        from datetime import date, timedelta
483	
484	        s = create_constant_surface(
485	            iv=0.25,
486	            as_of_date=date.today(),
487	            underlying="TESTA",
488	            spot=100.0,
489	            expiries=[date.today() + timedelta(days=d) for d in (7, 30, 60, 90)],
490	        )
491	        assert s is not None
492	        return "constant σ=0.25 surface built"
493	
494	    def skew_slope_check():
495	        out = skew_slope(iv_25d_put=0.28, iv_atm=0.24, iv_25d_call=0.22)
496	        assert "skew_slope" in out
497	        assert out["skew_slope"] > 0
498	        return f"slope={out['skew_slope']:.4f}"
499	
500	    def skew_momentum_check():
501	        slopes = np.linspace(0.0, 0.2, 30)
502	        mom = skew_momentum(slopes)
503	        assert isinstance(mom, dict) and len(mom) > 0
504	        return f"keys={list(mom.keys())[:3]}"
505	
506	    def ivs_dislocation():
507	        tenors = np.array([7 / 365, 30 / 365, 60 / 365, 90 / 365, 180 / 365, 365 / 365])
508	        ivs = np.array([0.28, 0.26, 0.25, 0.24, 0.23, 0.22])
509	        out = ivs_dislocation_score(tenors, ivs)
510	        assert isinstance(out, dict) and len(out) > 0
511	        return f"keys={list(out.keys())[:3]}"
512

553	    def hmm_fit_predict():
554	        log_rets = np.diff(np.log(ohlcv["close"].values))
555	        hmm = GaussianHMM(n_states=3, n_iter=20, random_state=42)
556	        hmm.fit(log_rets[-504:])
557	        probs = hmm.predict_proba(log_rets[-504:])
558	        assert probs.shape[1] == 3
559	        mult = hmm.position_multiplier(probs[-1])
560	        assert 0.0 <= mult <= 2.0
561	        return f"mult={mult:.3f}"
562

564	        rd = RegimeDetector()
565	        state = rd.detect_regime(current_iv=0.25, prices=ohlcv["close"])
566	        assert state is not None
567	        return f"regime={getattr(state, 'volatility_regime', '?')}"

939	    def dealer_analyze():

944	        )

955	    def dealer_multiplier():
956	        from datetime import date, timedelta
957	
958	        ms = DealerPositioningAnalyzer(assumption=DealerAssumption.LONG_CALLS_SHORT_PUTS).analyze(
959	            chain=chain,
960	            spot=100.0,
961	            expiry=date.today() + timedelta(days=32),
962	            ticker="TESTA",
963	            dividend_yield=0.0,
964	        )

966	        assert 0.70 <= m <= 1.05, m

969	    def gpd_fit_losses():
970	        rng = np.random.default_rng(0)
971	        losses = np.abs(rng.standard_t(4, 1500)) * 1.2
972	        fit = fit_gpd_tail(losses)
973	        if not fit.converged:
974	            raise Skip("GPD fit did not converge on synthetic")
975	        var99, cvar99 = gpd_var_cvar(fit, confidence=0.99)
976	        assert var99 > 0 and cvar99 >= var99
977	        return f"ξ={fit.shape_xi:.3f} CVaR99={cvar99:.3f}"
978

995	    def block_bootstrap_cls():

1000	        assert result is not None
1001	        return "BlockBootstrap simulated"

1003	    def jump_diffusion_sim():

1005	            JumpDiffusionParams(
1006	                mu=0.05,
1007	                sigma=0.2,
1008	                jump_intensity=0.1,
1009	                jump_mean=-0.02,
1010	                jump_std=0.05,
1011	                dividend_yield=0.0,
1012	            )
1013	        )
1014	        paths = sim.simulate_paths(S0=100.0, n_days=63)
1015	        assert paths.shape[-1] == 64 or paths.shape[0] >= 63
1016	        return f"shape={paths.shape}"

1018	    def lsm_american():

1021	            S0=100,
1022	            K=100,
1023	            T=30 / 365,
1024	            r=0.05,
1025	            sigma=0.3,
1026	            option_type="put",
1027	            q=0.0,
1028	        )
1029	        assert res.american_price > 0
1030	        return f"LSM am=${res.american_price:.3f} euro=${res.european_price:.3f}"

1096	    def gaussian_copula():
1097	        rng = np.random.default_rng(0)
1098	        marginals = [rng.normal(0, 0.01, 500), rng.normal(0, 0.01, 500)]
1099	        corr = np.array([[1.0, 0.5], [0.5, 1.0]])
1100	        sims = gaussian_copula_simulation(
1101	            marginals=marginals, correlation=corr, n_samples=5000, seed=0
1102	        )
1103	        assert sims.shape[1] == 2
1104	        return f"shape={sims.shape}"
1105	
1106	    def student_t_copula():
1107	        rng = np.random.default_rng(0)
1108	        marginals = [rng.normal(0, 0.01, 500), rng.normal(0, 0.01, 500)]
1109	        corr = np.array([[1.0, 0.5], [0.5, 1.0]])
1110	        sims = student_t_copula_simulation(
1111	            marginals=marginals, correlation=corr, df=4, n_samples=5000, seed=0
1112	        )
1113	        assert sims.shape[1] == 2
1114	        return f"df=4 shape={sims.shape}"

1116	    def portfolio_cvar():

1118	        marginals = [rng.normal(0, 0.01, 500) for _ in range(3)]
1119	        corr = np.array([[1.0, 0.3, 0.2], [0.3, 1.0, 0.4], [0.2, 0.4, 1.0]])
1120	        weights = np.array([1 / 3, 1 / 3, 1 / 3])
1121	        out = portfolio_cvar_copula(
1122	            marginals=marginals, correlation=corr, weights=weights, confidence=0.95, seed=0
1123	        )

1125	        return f"keys={list(out.keys())[:3]}"
